sizeEntry.name: Size:
entries: Medium (about 4-7 feet tall) or Small (about 2-4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Object Form
entries: As a mimic, you can switch back and forth between your natural form—a soft, fleshy purple body protected by chitinous plates—and three preferred object forms that resemble simple inanimate objects of your size, such as a treasure chest. Most mimics spend the majority of their time in a preferred object form, and you have perfected three such forms. As an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}, you can change your physical shape into your preferred object forms or back into your true form. The physical details of your preferred object forms are determined by you, but the forms are specific and unique, and you must change into the same forms each time you shift. While in your object form, you can speak and move as normal, though doing so will usually give away your true nature to anyone observing you. When you transform, you choose whether your equipment falls to the ground in your space, merges into your new form, or is worn by it. Your game statistics remain the same regardless of your form.

entries.name: Perfect Copy
entries: While you remain motionless in your object forms, you are indistinguishable from a normal version of the object you appear to be and magic may be able to reveal your true nature, and obviously creatures that watch you change into your object form know you are a mimic. Even if they can't tell for sure you are a mimic, if your object form stands out as unusual in your current location, other creatures might be wary or curious about your presence.

entries.name: Psuedopods
entries: Mimics don't have normal arms and hands, but in either your mimic or object form you are able to hold and manipulate objects using up two temporary pseudopods, which don't have hands or fingers. By default, your pseudopods work the same way as normal hands, though at the GM's discretion some actions might be easier for you and some more difficult. In much the same way, you are able to sprout a pair of legs, which you can use to walk even in your object form.

feat.name: Adhesive Grappler
entries: You can use every part of your body to grapple your foes, and when you grapple a creature, you cover them in adhesive, slowing them down for a short time after they escape. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike using your pseudopods (or your pseudopod blade, if you have the Mimic Blade feat), you can use a bonus action to attempt to grapple the target.

After a creature you have successfully {@condition grappled} is no longer {@condition grappled} (usually because you ended the grapple or the creature Escaped), the creature's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

feat.name: Chitinous Reinforcement
entries: You've learned to reinforce all of your forms with thicker chitin. While you are not wearing armor and using no shield, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC. This bonus isn't cumulative with {@item bracers of defense|XDMG}

feat.name: Glued Grip
entries: Like all adventuring mimics, you've altered your natural adhesive to allow you to move about more easily, but even so you've learned to use it selectively while in contact with the ground or when holding an object in your pseudopods. You gain the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on Strength saving throws against effects that would push or pull you, knock you {@condition prone}, or force an object out of your grip.

feat.name: Mimic Blade
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can transform one or both of your two pseudopods into a blade to attack your foes, or return your pseudopods back to their normal form. Until the next time you use Mimic Blade on a pseudopod, you can't hold or manipulate objects with that pseudopod, but in exchange for that restriction, you gain one of the following benefits:

items: When you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the pseudopod, you can deal Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Strength modifier, instead of the normal damage of an unarmed strike.

When you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the pseudopod, you can deal Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entries: If you choose, you can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls for the pseudopod.
Alternatively, you can choose one of the following benefits, which use both your pseudopods, meaning you can't hold or manipulate objects while using them:

items: When you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the pseudopod, the reach of your Unarmed Strikes increases by 5 feet, and you can deal Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Strength modifier, instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

When you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the pseudopod, you can deal Slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d10} + your Strength modifier, instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entries: Your pseudopod blades count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage

feat.name: Mimic Lore
entries: You either possess ancestral memories from the spores that formed you, or else you spent effort later in life to learn more about mimics.
You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in your choice of the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} or {@skill Nature|XPHB} skill, as well as your choice of the {@skill Athletics|XPHB} or {@skill Deception|XPHB} skill.

When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB} or {@skill Nature|XPHB}) check to recall information about mimics, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10. In addition, on a successful check you might know more information about the subject than normal, at the GM's discretion.

feat.name: Mimic Tongue
entries: You can extend your tongue out to a distance impossible for other creatures in order to quickly interact with an object or environmental feature. You gain the following benefits:

items: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can extend your tongue out to a great distance. You stretch out your tongue and interact with one object or feature of the environment within 30 feet, and then your tongue springs back into your mouth. This doesn't allow you to perform other actions at a distance with your tongue, such as attacks.

feat.name: Periscopic Eyes
entries: You can extend some of your eyes on stalks like periscopes. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can extend your eyes out as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Until your next turn, you can see around corners as if you were 5 feet ahead of you in a direction of your choice. Additionally, if you're using the flanking rules, creatures flanking you don't have advantage on attack rolls against you due to flanking

feat.name: Takes One to Know One
entries: As a mimic, you have an unsurpassed insight into creatures disguised as objects . You gain the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on all Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks to notice a creature disguised as an object, including other mimics, constructs holding still to appear as normal statues, and so on.

If you notice that a creature has disguised as an object before it reveals itself, you have advantage on your first attack against that creature, and that creature has disadvantage on their first attack against you.

feat.name: Adhesive Climb
entries: You can use the adhesive on your pseudopods to climb any surface with ease. You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to half your Walking {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. In addition, you can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

feat.name: Coin Shot
entries: You spit out and fire coins or other small objects you stored within your container-shaped body (or elsewhere if your body is shaped like a different object). You can use an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to make a ranged attack against a target you can see within 60 feet of you. Your bonus to the {@variantrule Attack Roll|XPHB} is equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your dexterity modifier.

The damage dealt by your coin shot attack is normally bludgeoning damage, but the GM might determine you deal piercing damage if you spit out something sufficiently spiky. Your coin shot attack deals {@damage 2d6} damage.

feat.name: Disguise Item
entries: You can use your talents at shaping yourself into objects in order to disguise another item, surrounding it with bits of yourself to create an illusory guise. As an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}, you can touch one nonmagical item that isn't being worn or carried and is no larger than a 10-foot cube.
You surround the object with a magical illusion that lasts for 1 hour.

The illusion must be roughly the same {@variantrule Size|XPHB} and shape as the original object, but otherwise the details of the illusion are up to you. You can make the object look decrepit, appearing broken and shoddy. You can make it look perfect, appearing as though it's brand new and highly polished or well maintained. You can also make other similar disguises, as determined by the GM.
The illusion fails to hold up to physical inspection. For example, if you use this ability to make a chipped sword appear new, anyone running their thumb along the blade would feel the imperfections.

Otherwise, a creature can attempt to see through the illusion by using an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against a DC equal to 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Charisma modifier.
Once you have used this ability, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can do so again

feat.name: Erase Memories
entries: You can erase memories that you find boring, useless, or problematic, allowing you to remain sessile for long periods of time without feeling the monotony quite as strongly. In addition to its mundane uses, this ability allows you to erase memories of traumatic or debilitating mental experiences. When you start your turn {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, you can immediately repeat the saving throw against the initial DC of the effect (or DC 20 if there was no initial DC). On a successful save, the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition ends.
Once you have used this ability, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can do so again.

feat.name: Jump Scare
entries: When an adjacent creature believes you are a normal object, rather than a mimic, you can scare them as an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} by suddenly revealing that you're a mimic. The creature must attempt a Will saving throw with a DC of 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failure, they're {@condition Frightened|XPHB} until the end of their next turn.

feat.name: Mimic Object
entries: With some effort, you can use your {@variantrule Object|XPHB} Form species trait to transform into any object, not just one of your preferred object forms.

The physical details of this form are determined by you at the time you transform. As normal, the object must be a simple inanimate object of your size, and your game statistics remain the same in your new form.
Once you have used this ability to transform into an object other than one of your preferred object forms, you must finish a short rest before you can do so again

feat.name: Object Lesson
entries: When a creature that believes you are a normal object, rather than a mimic, touches or physically interacts with you, you can use your

{@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using your pseudopod against that creature. You have advantage on the attack roll, and on a hit you deal an extra ({@dice 2d6}) damage. This extra damage increases by {@dice 2d6} at 8th ({@dice 4d6}), 12th ({@dice 6d6}), and 16th ({@dice 8d6}) level

feat.name: Object Reader
entries: Your time spent studying and transforming into objects has granted you a mystical ability to understand them and experience the emotional auras dyed into the object's history. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can place a hand on an object as an action in order to learn a piece of information about an emotional event that occurred involving the object some time within the past week. When you first use {@variantrule Object|XPHB} Reader on an object, you don't choose the topic of information you receive, and you can't choose the event in question if there were multiple emotional events in the last week. However, if you use your {@variantrule Object|XPHB} Reader feat on the the same item multiple times, you can either choose to focus on an event further to gain additional pieces of information about that same event, or to move on to gain a piece of information about a different emotional event within the applicable time frame. If you choose to move on to another event, you still can't choose the new event

feat.name: Trapper Mimic
entries: You've learned how to set traps to protect your lair. As an action, you can coat the ground in a 10-foot square within 5 feet of you with a subtle, sticky adhesive. The adhesive is nearly {@condition invisible} and requires a successful Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier to be noticed.
The adhesive trap you create on the ground lasts for 10 minutes.

While the adhesive lasts, the area is difficult terrain to all creatures except you. In addition, a creature that enters the area or starts its turn inside the area must make a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier. On a failed save, the creature becomes stuck and is {@condition restrained} by the adhesive for as long as the adhesive lasts. A creature {@condition restrained} by your trap or adjacent to a {@condition restrained} creature can use an action to make a Strength check against your DC for this ability, freeing themselves or an adjacent {@condition restrained} creature on a success. Once a creature has successfully escaped (or been freed), they are immune to your adhesive for 24 hours.
Once you have used this ability, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

feat.name: Additional Forms
entries: You can shapeshift into unfamiliar objects more often. Instead of being able to use your Mimic {@variantrule Object|XPHB} feat only once between short rests, you can now use it any number of times.

feat.name: Corrosive Grasp
entries: Your most powerful attacks spatter corrosive adhesive on your foes, {@condition burning|MonstersOfDrakkenheim} them with acid. When you score a critical hit with an Unarmed

Strike using your pseudopod (or your pseudopod blade, if you have the Mimic Blade feat), the target takes an extra {@damage 2d4} acid damage immediately and {@damage 2d4} acid damage at the end of its next turn

feat.name: Devour Treasure
entries: You forge a connection between yourself and a treasure chest on the Ethereal Plane, allowing you to gorge on treasure and send it there, or recall the treasure back yourself. You can spend 1 minute to devour up to 12 cubic feet of nonliving material (3 feet by 2 feet by 2 feet) and send it to the deepest reaches of the Ethereal Plane. The material remains on the Ethereal Plane until you use an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to recall the material, whereupon it instantly appears in a special cavity you have created inside your own body.
After 60 days, there is a cumulative {@chance 5} chance per day that the material is irrevocably lost in a random location somewhere deep on the Ethereal Plane

feat.name: Rejuvenating Gorge
entries: You are able to repair yourself and replace damaged flesh by gorging on unusually large amounts of food. During a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can consume up to 7 days worth of food to regain hit points equal to your Constitution modifier × the number of days' worth of food you consumed. At 12th level, you can eat up to 10 days worth of food, and at 16th level you can eat up to 14 days worth of food. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, after which you must take a long rest before you can recover hit points via a rejuvenating gorge again.

feat.name: Total Mimicry
entries: Using a process similar to your normal object mimicry, only more complete, you can transform yourself fully into an object, rather than just a facsimile with the appearance of the object. As an {@variantrule Action|XPHB}, you can become an object of your size or smaller made of vegetable matter or common minerals. The object you become can't rely on intricate artistry or complex moving parts, can't be used as a material component for a spell, and can't be made of precious materials such as gold or gemstone.

While in this form, others can use you as the intended item. However, you have no sensory organs and can't perceive the world around you other than to sense when others touch you. You can't take actions other than to use an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to return to your normal form. You can condense yourself into an object with less volume than your normal form, but your mass remains the same, so if you become a particularly small object, your extremely high density might give you away.
Once you have used this ability, you must finish a short rest before you can do so again.

feat.name: Vigilant Guardian
entries: You can enter a state of near-hibernation to pass long periods of time without boredom, but you're always vigilant to act the moment a creature invades your territory or campsite. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you're sleeping normally (not via a sleep spell or a similar effect), you are still aware of your surroundings.

You can spend 10 minutes winding down your biological processes in order to enter a state of near-hibernation. In this state, you don't need to eat or drink, and you age extremely slowly. Your mind calms down and mostly empties of conscious thought, which helps you avoid boredom, but you're still aware enough of your surroundings to exit your hibernation if an intruder or visitor arrives. You can exit your hibernation as a bonus action.

feat.name: Hulking Mimic
entries: In a process that takes roughly 24 hours, you gorge yourself on over a ton of food and enter into a deep, transformative slumber. During this time, your body surges in size. When you awaken, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your size becomes Large. While you are Large, when you use your {@variantrule Object|XPHB} Form species trait, you can transform only into

entries: Large objects.

items: When you hit a target with an unarmed strike using your pseudopods (or your pseudopod blade, if you have the Mimic Blade feat), you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} damage

feat.name: Interior Camouflage
entries: Your ability to alter your shape, texture, and coloration improves to become reactive as you move, allowing you to camouflage yourself inside normal buildings with ease. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action in most normal buildings even when other creatures can see you. In addition, you gain advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks so long as you remain in one spot without moving or taking actions. You can't use this ability to hide in natural environments, and the GM may decide that you can't hide in buildings with exceptionally unusual decor.

feat.name: Internal Plating
entries: You've developed additional chitin plates inside your body and can move them to unexpected places to protect your vitals from critical strikes. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain resistance to piercing damage.
When you are subjected to a critical hit, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 10 or higher, the critical hit becomes a normal hit instead

feat.name: Trapper Elite
entries: Your ability to set traps is a legend, and people tell worried stories about the dangers of challenging your lair. You gain the following benefits:

items: Instead of being able to use your Trapper Mimic feat only once between long rests, you can now use it a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
When you use your Trapper Mimic feat, you can launch the adhesive from your body, coating the ground in a 10-foot square that you can see within 60 feet of you.

feat.name: Corrosive Adhesive
entries: As a bonus action, you can transform the adhesive that coats your body into corrosive acid. Your adhesive remains in this form for 1 minute, until you fall {@condition unconscious}, or until you use a bonus action to return your adhesive to normal. While covered in corrosive adhesive, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have immunity to acid damage.
When you hit a target with an unarmed strike using your pseudopods (or your pseudopod blade, if you have the Mimic Blade feat), you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} acid damage.
When a creature within 5 feet of you touches you or hits you with a melee attack, that creature takes {@damage 2d6} acid damage.

entries: Once you have used this ability, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

feat.name: Insidious Mimic
entries: It's almost impossible for anyone to determine you're actually a mimic, even using magic. When you use your {@variantrule Object|XPHB} Form species trait to adopt a form other than your natural form, you become immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, learn your alignment or creature type, or otherwise discern your true form.
